name: Prefab Manufacturing Building
description:
Prefab Manufacturing Buildings — A Production Floor Built for Machinery

Prefab manufacturing buildings have one job your office, showroom, or storage shed does not: they have to carry the floor loads, clear span, and utility runs a working production line demands. SAMAN builds that as a single steel-and-wood structure — 90 × 36 ft, 14 ft to the eaves — fabricated at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and delivered ready to take machinery. This page covers what that building gives a factory buyer, what it costs, and how to tell it apart from an industrial or commercial prefab.

A SAMAN prefab manufacturing building sited in a working industrial estate, set up for production.

What a production floor needs from the building — load, span and clear height

A building meant for production is specified backwards from the machines that will stand on its floor. Three things matter before anything else. Floor load: a manufacturing floor carries concentrated point loads from presses, CNC beds, compressors, and forklift traffic — far heavier than an office or retail slab is ever designed for, so the floor and footings are sized to the equipment schedule you supply. Clear span: the 90 × 36 ft footprint gives a 36 ft clear span with no internal columns interrupting the bay, so you lay machines and material flow to suit the process rather than working around the structure. Clear height: 14 ft to the eaves leaves room for tall equipment, gantry movement, stacked work-in-progress, and overhead crane provision where a line needs it.

The 36 ft clear span lets machines and material flow follow the process, not the structure.

Machinery-ready: power, ventilation, drainage and mezzanine options

A production hall is only useful once power and services reach the machines. SAMAN runs the building to take distributed power to machine positions rather than a single domestic point, and the steel-and-wood envelope accepts wall and roof openings for ventilation and heat or fume extraction over hot processes. Wet or washdown processes can be planned with floor drainage from the start.

A mezzanine adds QC, stores or office space above the floor; services run to each machine position.

Where you need quality-control, stores, or a supervisor's cabin without giving up floor area, a mezzanine can be added inside the same shell — useful office and inspection space sitting above the production floor.

How long does it take to build a prefab manufacturing building?

A prefab manufacturing building is fabricated off-site while your groundwork — flooring, foundations, and power supply — runs in parallel, so the structure itself is erected in a fraction of conventional time. A standard 90 × 36 ft SAMAN unit is typically ready for handover in around 30 to 45 working days, against the several months an equivalent RCC factory shell would take to cast and cure.

Off-site fabrication plus parallel groundwork — the structure goes up in a fraction of RCC time.

Sizing it to your line — inside the 90 × 36 ft production hall

The standard build gives you 3,240 sq ft of column-free production floor — wide enough to run two machine rows with a central material aisle, or a single heavy line with side clearance for handling. At 14 ft clear height it suits most light-to-medium manufacturing, assembly, packing, and processing work. The 90 × 36 × 14 ft configuration is priced at ₹33,55,000 (regular ₹33,75,000) as a steel-and-wood structure. Larger floor areas, wider spans, taller eaves for crane duty, or additional bays are quoted to your machine layout — the standard unit is a starting point, not a ceiling.

Manufacturing, industrial or commercial prefab — matching the build to your operation

Three prefab building types in our range look similar from outside but are specified for different work. Picking by the work you do — not the look — keeps you from over- or under-building.

A manufacturing unit sits among industrial and storage neighbours — different builds for different work.

Build type Best for Floor & layout priority
Manufacturing Making or processing products — production lines, machine shops, assembly Heavy floor load, wide clear span, crane height
Industrial Warehousing, storage, logistics, general industrial use Volume, racking and dock access
Commercial Retail, showrooms, service centres, customer-facing offices Frontage, finish, customer access

If your priority is storing and moving goods rather than making them, an industrial build is the closer fit; if customers walk in, look at the prefab commercial buildings for retail and showrooms. For the full range across every building format, see SAMAN's prefab building range.

Get a Quote for Your Prefab Manufacturing Building

Every manufacturing building is built to your equipment schedule and erected on your site by SAMAN. Share your machine list, plot size, and power requirement for a price covering the floor, span, height, and services to match.

Bangalore: Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859

Delhi NCR: Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937

FAQs

How is a prefab manufacturing building different from a prefab industrial building?

A manufacturing building is specified around making products — heavy floor loads under machinery, a wide column-free span for the production line, and clear height for equipment and cranes. An industrial building is specified around storing and moving goods — volume, racking, and dock access. Same prefab method, different priorities; choose by whether you're producing or storing.

What floor load and clear height does the 90 × 36 ft building offer for heavy machinery?

The standard unit gives a 36 ft column-free clear span and 14 ft clear height to the eaves, with the floor and footings sized to the equipment schedule you provide. Point loads from presses, CNC beds, and forklift traffic are designed in rather than assumed.

Can a prefab manufacturing building be relocated if my factory is on a leased plot?

Yes. It is a bolt-together steel-and-wood structure, so it can be dismantled and re-erected at a new site — a practical advantage when production runs on a leased industrial plot with a fixed lease term.

How much does a prefab manufacturing building cost in India?

The 90 × 36 × 14 ft SAMAN unit is ₹33,55,000 (regular ₹33,75,000). Cost moves with floor area, span width, eaves height, and the services your process needs, so a building sized to your machine layout is quoted on request.
